# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 127

This is turn 127. Turns 1 through 126 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 127.

## Turn 127 — the nested proper-image correction collapses to a one-level certificate

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit, and the proof-level exponent is the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); \(40491355905\) is its prescribed verifier residue. 

Turn 126 reduced the proof to a source-independent triangular count functional, but its terminal rank-one recurrence still contains the nested correction \(P(B)\).  That nesting can be removed exactly.

Let
\[
\pi_R:Y\longrightarrow B=Y/R,\qquad
\pi_K:Y\longrightarrow C=Y/K,
\]
in the branch
\[
R=\Phi(K)\cong C_2,\qquad \operatorname{edge}(\lambda)=0.
\]

Turn 124 defined
\[
P(B)=
\sum_{\substack{J<B\\J\twoheadrightarrow C}}
\ \sum_{\substack{J'\leq \pi_R^{-1}(J)\\
                   \pi_R(J')=J}}
E(J').
\]

There is no genuine double incidence here. The map
\[
(J,J')\longmapsto J'
\]
is a bijection from the indexing pairs onto
\[
\mathcal P(Y;B,C)
=
\left\{
J'\leq Y:
\pi_K(J')=C,\quad \pi_R(J')<B
\right\}.
\]

Indeed, \(J\) is necessarily and uniquely \(\pi_R(J')\). Conversely, every \(J'\) in this displayed set lies in the inverse image of its proper \(B\)-image, which maps onto \(C\). Therefore
\[
P(B)=
\sum_{\substack{J'\leq Y\\
                 \pi_K(J')=C\\
                 \pi_R(J')<B}}
E(J').
\tag{127.1}
\]

Every subgroup in (127.1) still has marked-kernel order at most \(|L_Y|/2\): its image in \(B\) is a proper subgroup mapping onto \(C\), so its intersection with \(M=K/R\) is proper; lifting back across \(R\cong C_2\) restores at most one factor of two.

Consequently the terminal recurrence becomes the genuinely one-level formula
\[
\boxed{
\begin{aligned}
E(Y)=&\ |T_0|^2q^3
\left[
\varepsilon
\sum_{a\in (T_0^\vee)^C}S_a(C)
+
4\bigl(q-\varepsilon d_0\bigr)E(C)
\right]\\
&-
\sum_{\substack{J'<Y\\
                 \pi_K(J')=C\\
                 \pi_R(J')<B}}
E(J'),
\end{aligned}}
\tag{127.2}
\]
where
\[
q=\sqrt{|V|},\qquad
d_0=\left|(T_0^\vee)^C\right|,
\qquad
\varepsilon=
\begin{cases}
-1,&V\text{ unramified},\\
+1,&V\text{ ramified}.
\end{cases}
\]

This also removes two manuscript ambiguities: the source-free count should be denoted \(\mathfrak E\), reserving \(\operatorname{edge}(\lambda)\) for the radical-edge map, and \(q\) must explicitly mean \(\sqrt{|V|}\), not \(|V|\).

### Finite certificate theorem

For each boundary-framed marked target \(Y\), construct a rooted certificate as follows.

A terminal node records the pullback decomposition
\[
Y\cong H\times_{H_2}Q
\]
and its compatible exact-image count from the common maximal pro-\(2\) group \(\Pi\).

A nonterminal node records one admissible active block \(K\), its branch, and precisely the child targets appearing in the appropriate recurrence:

\[
\begin{array}{c|c}
\text{branch}&\text{children}\\ \hline
R=1&C\text{ and proper }J<Y\text{ mapping onto }C,\\
|R|\ge4&B\text{ and the subgroups of each }Y_\lambda\text{ above }B,\\
R=C_2,\ \operatorname{edge}\ne0&B,\\
R=C_2,\ \operatorname{edge}=0&
C,\ C^{\Delta_a}\text{-subgroups above }C,\text{ and the }J'\text{ of (127.1)}.
\end{array}
\]

Every child has marked-kernel order at most half that of its parent. Hence the certificate depth is at most
\[
\left\lceil\log_2|L_Y|\right\rceil.
\]

Bottom-up evaluation using (125.1)–(125.3) and (127.2) produces \(\mathfrak E(Y)\). The triangular-rigidity argument then gives
\[
e_{\Gamma_A}(Y)=\mathfrak E(Y)=e_{G_{\mathbb Q_2}}(Y).
\]

Thus the global proof can be represented by a finite, locally checkable target certificate. No pairing of candidate and local lower maps, confluence theorem, or nested proper-image recursion remains. The only analytic label is the already-matched determinant sign \(\varepsilon\); everything else is finite subgroup, module, and central-cover data.

**ESTABLISHED**: Collapsed the nested correction \(P(B)\) to the single subgroup-incidence sum (127.1), yielding the one-level terminal recurrence (127.2). Formulated a finite active-block certificate theorem: every target count is obtained from a depth-\(\lceil\log_2|L|\rceil\) tree whose children have at most half the marked-kernel order.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun, and the determinant-sign and active-block lemmas still require independent line-by-line review in the final manuscript.

**NEXT**: Rewrite the final global theorem using the four one-level recurrences, the explicit certificate schema, and the notation \(\mathfrak E\); then freeze and submit the unchanged candidate block.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
